spec_match.rationale: The focusgroup side uses focusgroup="radiogroup block nomemory" with tabindex="0" custom radio candidates, matching the deep descendant focusability pattern.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the old panel handles End and ArrowLeft with synchronized tabindex and aria-checked state, while the focusgroup panel moves to the next custom radio and updates state on Space.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The explanatory text distinguishes focus management from widget selection state and no longer implies focusgroup owns aria-checked.

edge_cases.rationale: MCP verified the custom div focusgroup is a single Tab stop, preserves focusgroupstart on selection, and emits no console errors.

references.rationale: The page cites the ChromeStatus feature and current scoped Open UI explainer.

design_system.rationale: Existing borders, focus outlines, variable colors, and comparison layout remain intact.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP verification used Chrome Canary 151 with experimental web platform features enabled. The custom radio focusgroup was one Tab stop, arrow navigation and Space selection worked, and console/network checks were clean.
